title: Dataiku DSS API overview
text:
API: Dataiku DSS API

Call paths:
- All calls in the DSS public API are relative to the /public/api path on the DSS server.
- For example, if your DSS server is available at http://mymachine:10000/, and you want to call the List projects API, you must make a GET request on http://mymachine:10000/public/api/projects/
- curl --header 'Authorization: Bearer <USER_API_KEY>' -H "Content-Type: application/json" -X GET http://mymachine:port/public/api/projects/

Other notes:
- Many API calls may return additional attributes compared to the documentation. The stability of these additional attributes is not guaranteed. They may be modified or removed in future releases without notice.
- All API calls will return the following two HTTP headers
- DSS-API-Version: Version of the API server handling the request DSS-Version : Version of the DSS backend answering the request

POST /project-folders/{folderId}/move{?destination} - Move a project folder
text:
Endpoint: Move a project folder
Method: POST
Path: /project-folders/{folderId}/move{?destination}
Module: Project Folders
Resource: Project Folder
Summary: Move a project folder into another project folder (change its parent) along with its content. You cannot move a project folder into one of its sub-folder.
Example URI: /project-folders/KdLmPU6/move?destination=dgKywsx
Required privileges: Required privileges : ADMIN on this project folder, WRITE_CONTENTS on the destination project folder
Response codes: 204
URI parameters:
- folderId (string, required). the ID of a project folder to move The folderId can be found from the get project folder API call or in the URL when accessing DSS GUI.. Example: KdLmPU6
- destination (string, required). the ID of the destination project folder The destination (project folder ID) can be found from the get project folder API call or in the URL when accessing DSS GUI.. Example: dgKywsx
Response details available: 204[no-body]

title: DELETE /project-folders/{folderId} - Delete project
text:
Endpoint: Delete project
Method: DELETE
Path: /project-folders/{folderId}
Module: Project Folders
Resource: Project Folder
Summary: Permanently deletes a project folder. It must be empty: no sub-folders and no projects inside
Example URI: /project-folders/KdLmPU6
Required privileges: Required privileges : ADMIN (on this project folder)
Response codes: 204
URI parameters:
- folderId (string, required). the key of the project folder to delete The folderId can be found from the get project folder API call or in the URL when accessing DSS GUI.. Example: KdLmPU6
Response details available: 204[no-body]

title: POST /project-folders/{folderId}/children{?name} - Create a sub-project folder
text:
Endpoint: Create a sub-project folder
Method: POST
Path: /project-folders/{folderId}/children{?name}
Module: Project Folders
Resource: Project Folder
Summary: Create a sub-project folder within the current project folder.
Example URI: /project-folders/KdLmPU6/children?name=my_sub_folder
Required privileges: Required privileges : WRITE_CONTENTS on the current project folder
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- folderId (string, required). the ID of a project folder to move The folderId can be found from the get project folder API call or in the URL when accessing DSS GUI.. Example: KdLmPU6
- name (string, required). the name of the new project folder. Example: my_sub_folder
Response details available: 200[headers,body,schema]

POST /project-folders/{folderId}/projects/{projectKey}/move{?destination} - Move a project
text:
Endpoint: Move a project
Method: POST
Path: /project-folders/{folderId}/projects/{projectKey}/move{?destination}
Module: Project Folders
Resource: Project Folder
Summary: Move a project within the current project folder to another project folder
Example URI: /project-folders/KdLmPU6/projects/MYPROJECT/move?destination=dgKywsx
Required privileges: Required privileges : WRITE_CONTENTS on the destination project folder, Admin of project
Response codes: 204
URI parameters:
- folderId (string, required). the ID of a project folder where is the project is The folderId can be found from the get project folder API call or in the URL when accessing DSS GUI.. Example: KdLmPU6
- projectKey (string, required). the ID of the project to move The projectKey can be found with the list projects API call or in the URL when accessing DSS GUI.. Example: MYPROJECT
- destination (string, required). the ID of the destination project folder The destination (project folder ID) can be found from the get project folder API call or in the URL when accessing DSS GUI.. Example: dgKywsx
Response details available: 204[no-body]

DELETE /projects/{projectKey}{?dropData} - Delete project
text:
Endpoint: Delete project
Method: DELETE
Path: /projects/{projectKey}{?dropData}
Module: Projects
Resource: Project
Summary: Permanently deletes a project and all its associated datasets, recipes, models, etc.
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT?dropData=
Required privileges: Required privileges : ADMIN (on this project)
Response codes: 204
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of the project to delete NB The project key is usually different than the project displayed name. The projectKey can be found with the list projects API call or in the URL when accessing DSS GUI.. Example: MYPROJECT
- dropData (boolean, optional). Drop the data from any managed datasets
Response details available: 204[no-body]

title: GET /projects/{projectKey}/export{?exportUploads}{?exportManaged}{?exportAnalysisModels}{?exportSavedModels} - Export project
text:
Endpoint: Export project
Method: GET
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/export{?exportUploads}{?exportManaged}{?exportAnalysisModels}{?exportSavedModels}
Module: Projects
Resource: Project
Summary: Exports a whole project configuration and (optionally) its associated data.
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/export?exportUploads=?exportManaged=?exportAnalysisModels=?exportSavedModels=
Required privileges: Required privileges : ADMIN (on this project)
Note: Only the content of Managed Filesystem datasets and uploaded datasets can be exported.
Note: The returned zip archive can be used in DSS import feature.
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project NB The project key is usually different than the project displayed name. The projectKey can be found with the list projects API call or in the URL when accessing DSS GUI.. Example: MYPROJECT
- exportUploads (boolean, optional). Export uploaded datasets data
- exportManaged (boolean, optional). Export managed Filesystem datasets data
- exportAnalysisModels (boolean, optional). Export trained models that are in analyses (not deployed in the flow)
- exportSavedModels (boolean, optional). Export saved models (deployed in the flow)
Response details available: 200[headers]

title: POST /projects/{projectKey}/actions/push-to-git-remote{?remote} - Push to Git remote
text:
Endpoint: Push to Git remote
Method: POST
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/actions/push-to-git-remote{?remote}
Module: Projects
Resource: Project
Summary: Pushes the content of a project to a previously-declared Git remote. The remote must have been added first in the “Changes” section of DSS.
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/actions/push-to-git-remote?remote=origin
Required privileges: Required privileges : ADMIN (on this project)
Note: DSS must be in “per-project Git” mode
Response codes: 204
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project NB The project key is usually different than the project displayed name. The projectKey can be found with the list projects API call or in the URL when accessing DSS GUI.. Example: MYPROJECT
- remote (string, required). The remote name to push to. Example: origin
Response details available: 204[no-body]

Endpoint: Get Data Quality project timeline
Method: GET
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/data-quality/timeline{?minTimestamp}{?maxTimestamp}
Module: Data Quality
Resource: Project
Summary: Get a detailed data quality status of each dataset on the project grouped per day between the specified minTimestamp and maxTimestamp. If the optional parameters are not specified it will include the results for the last 14 days.
Example URI: /projects/MY_PROJECT/data-quality/timeline?minTimestamp=?maxTimestamp=
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the id of a Project The Project id can be found with list Project API call or in the URL when accessing DSS GUI.. Example: MY_PROJECT
- minTimestamp (number, optional). The beginning of the timeframe for the timeline
- maxTimestamp (number, optional). The end of the timeframe for the timeline
Response details available: 200[headers,body,schema]

endpoint_name: Get last outcome on specific partition
text:
Endpoint: Get last outcome on specific partition
Method: POST
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/data-quality/get-partitions-status
Module: Data Quality
Resource: Dataset
Summary: Get the last status of the computed rules on a specific partition. If the dataset is not partitioned, no need to specify anything it will return the status of the dataset.
Example URI: /projects/MY_PROJECT/datasets/MY_DATASET/data-quality/get-partitions-status
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the id of a Project. Example: MY_PROJECT
- datasetName (string, required). the id of a dataset on the project. Example: MY_DATASET
- partitions (array[string], optional). The partitions to get the status from. Optional when the dataset is not partitioned
Response details available: 200[headers,body,schema]

title: POST /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/data-quality/actions/compute-rules - Compute rules on a specific partition
endpoint_name: Compute rules on a specific partition
text:
Endpoint: Compute rules on a specific partition
Method: POST
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/data-quality/actions/compute-rules
Module: Data Quality
Resource: Dataset
Summary: Compute all the enabled rules on the dataset on a specific partition. If the dataset is not partitioned, no need to specify anything. Returns a reference to a future, defined by its jobId.
Example URI: /projects/MY_PROJECT/datasets/MY_DATASET/data-quality/actions/compute-rules
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the id of a Project. Example: MY_PROJECT
- datasetName (string, required). the id of a dataset on the project. Example: MY_DATASET
- partition (string, optional). The partition to get the status from. Optional when the dataset is not partitioned
- ruleId (string, optional). If you want only one specific rule to be run. You need to pass its rule id. If nothing is set all the rules will be run
Response details available: 200[headers,body]

title: GET /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/data-quality/status-by-partition{?includeAllPartitions} - Get current status of the dataset per partitions
endpoint_name: Get current status of the dataset per partitions
text:
Endpoint: Get current status of the dataset per partitions
Method: GET
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/data-quality/status-by-partition{?includeAllPartitions}
Module: Data Quality
Resource: Dataset
Summary: Get the detailed status of a dataset per partition. The result will contain at most 1 result with the name “NP” (Not Partitioned) for non-partitioned dataset. It returns an object having partition name as keys with their status results.
Example URI: /projects/MY_PROJECT/datasets/MY_DATASET/data-quality/status-by-partition?includeAllPartitions=
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the id of a Project. Example: MY_PROJECT
- datasetName (string, required). the id of a dataset on the project. Example: MY_DATASET
- includeAllPartitions (boolean, optional). Include all the last partitions status ever run. If false it will return only the partitions taken into account for the dataset current status
Response details available: 200[headers,body,schema]

title: GET /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/data-quality/last-rules-result{?partition}{?ruleId} - Get the last rule results
text:
Endpoint: Get the last rule results
Method: GET
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/data-quality/last-rules-result{?partition}{?ruleId}
Module: Data Quality
Resource: Rules
Summary: Get the last result of all the rules defined on the dataset on a specified partition. If the dataset is not partitioned it will get all the last rules results.
Example URI: /projects/MY_PROJECT/datasets/MY_DATASET/data-quality/last-rules-result?partition=?ruleId=
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the id of a Project. Example: MY_PROJECT
- datasetName (string, required). the id of a dataset on the project. Example: MY_DATASET
- partition (string, optional). The partition to get the results from. Optional when the dataset is not partitioned
- ruleId (string, optional). The rule id to get the last result from. If not specified it will fetch all the last rules result defined on the dataset
Response details available: 200[headers,body,schema]

GET /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/data-quality/rules-history{?minTimestamp}{?maxTimestamp}{?resultsPerPage}{?page}{?ruleIds} - Get the rule history
text:
Endpoint: Get the rule history
Method: GET
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/data-quality/rules-history{?minTimestamp}{?maxTimestamp}{?resultsPerPage}{?page}{?ruleIds}
Module: Data Quality
Resource: Rules
Summary: Get the history of computed rules on the dataset matching the specifier timeframe and filters if any.
Example URI: /projects/MY_PROJECT/datasets/MY_DATASET/data-quality/rules-history?minTimestamp=?maxTimestamp=?resultsPerPage=?page=?ruleIds=
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the id of a Project. Example: MY_PROJECT
- datasetName (string, required). the id of a dataset on the project. Example: MY_DATASET
- minTimestamp (number, optional). The beginning of the timeframe for the timeline
- maxTimestamp (number, optional). The end of the timeframe for the timeline
- resultsPerPage (number, optional). The number of results to be returned. Default value will return the last 10 000 results
- page (number, optional). The offset on the results to be returned. Default will be no offset
- ruleIds (string, optional). A list of rules id to get the rule history from. If not specified it will fetch all the rules result defined on the dataset
Response details available: 200[headers,body,schema]

text:

Method: PUT

Module: Data Quality
Resource: Rules
Summary: Update a rule on a dataset with a specified configuration. If you want to create a new rule, please use POST /data-quality/project/{projectKey}/dataset/{datasetName}/create-rule.
Example URI: /projects/MY_PROJECT/datasets/MY_DATASET/data-quality/rules/32fZlgA
Note: Note that depending on the type of rule you are updating you might want to specify other type-fields dependant than the one specified in the request body example.
Response codes: 201
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the id of a Project. Example: MY_PROJECT
- datasetName (string, required). the id of a dataset on the project. Example: MY_DATASET
- ruleId (string, required). the id of the rule to be modified.. Example: 32fZlgA

title: POST /projects/{projectKey}/flow/documentation/generate - Generate flow documentation from default template
resource_name: Generate flow documentation from default template
endpoint_name: Generate flow documentation from default template
text:
Endpoint: Generate flow documentation from default template
Method: POST
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/flow/documentation/generate
Module: Flow
Resource: Generate flow documentation from default template
Summary: Start the flow documentation generation using the default template. Returns a reference to a future, defined by its jobId. If the future has no result yet, polling on the jobId with GET task until the result is ready is needed to get the id of the generated flow documentation.
Example URI: /projects/projectKey/flow/documentation/generate
Required privileges: Required privileges : READ_CONF on the project
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project
Response details available: 200[headers,body]

title: GET /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{?tags}{?foreign} - List datasets
text:
Endpoint: List datasets
Method: GET
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{?tags}{?foreign}
Module: Datasets
Resource: Datasets
Summary: Lists the datasets of a project.
Example URI: /projects/projectKey/datasets/?tags=tag1,tag2?foreign=true
Required privileges: Required privileges : READ_CONF
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of the project
- foreign (boolean, optional). If true, also lists the datasets from other projects that are exposed to the specified project. Example: true
- tags (string, optional). Comma separated list of tags. The query will only return datasets having one of these tags. Example: tag1,tag2
Response details available: 200[headers,body,schema]

title: GET /projects/datasets/column-lineage{?columnName}{?maxDatasetCount} - Get column lineage
text:
Endpoint: Get column lineage
Method: GET
Path: /projects/datasets/column-lineage{?columnName}{?maxDatasetCount}
Module: Datasets
Resource: Dataset Column Lineage
Summary: Retrieves the full lineage (auto-computed and manual) information of a column in a specified dataset. Column relations with datasets from both local and foreign projects will be included in the result.
Example URI: /projects/datasets/column-lineage?columnName=mycol1?maxDatasetCount=2000
Required privileges: Required privileges : READ_DATA on Dataset
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- columnName (string, required). the name of a column. Example: mycol1
- maxDatasetCount (integer, optional). the maximum number of datasets to query for. By default, the max hard limit is used. Example: 2000
Response details available: 200[body]

- POST /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/data :: Get data - alternative version

title: GET /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/data{?format}{?formatParams}{?columns}{?partitions}{?filter}{?sampling} - Get data
text:
Endpoint: Get data
Method: GET
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/data{?format}{?formatParams}{?columns}{?partitions}{?filter}{?sampling}
Module: Datasets
Resource: Dataset data
Summary: Streams the content of a dataset.
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/datasets/mydataset/data?format=json?formatParams=?columns=mycol1,mycol2?partitions=2015-07-07?filter=mycol1 > 0 && mycol3 > 0?sampling=
Required privileges: Required privileges : READ_DATA on Dataset
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project. Example: MYPROJECT
- datasetName (string, required). the name of a dataset. Example: mydataset
- format (string, optional). Output format name. Supported formats are : “json”, “tsv-excel-header” (tab-separated with header) and “tsv-excel-noheader” (tab-separated without header). Example: json
- formatParams (string, optional). additional parameters for the chosen format, as a json string
- columns (string, optional). List of requested columns, as a comma-separated list. Example: mycol1,mycol2
- sampling (string, optional). definition of a sampling to use when retrieving the data. By default, no sampling (returns all data)
- partitions (string, optional). Partition list specification. Example: 2015-07-07
- filter (string, optional). Formula to select only a subset of rows based on a boolean expression. Example: mycol1 > 0 && mycol3 > 0
Response details available: 200[body]

title: DELETE /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/data/{?partitions} - Clear data
text:
Endpoint: Clear data
Method: DELETE
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/data/{?partitions}
Module: Datasets
Resource: Dataset data
Summary: Clears the data contained in the dataset; the dataset itself is not deleted.
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/datasets/mydataset/data/?partitions=NP
Required privileges: Required privileges : WRITE_DATA
Note: If a list of partition identifiers is specified, only the corresponding partitions are cleared.
Response codes: 204
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project. Example: MYPROJECT
- datasetName (string, required). the name of a dataset. Example: mydataset
- partitions (string, optional). List of partitions to clear, as a partitions spec. Example: NP
Response details available: 204[no-body]

text:
Endpoint: Get last metric values
Method: GET
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/metrics/last/{?partition}
Module: Datasets
Resource: Dataset status
Summary: Retrieve the last values of all metrics on a given dataset (or dataset partition)
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/datasets/mydataset/metrics/last/?partition=2015-02-03
Required privileges: Required privileges : READ_METADATA
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project. Example: MYPROJECT
- datasetName (string, required). the name of a dataset. Example: mydataset
- partition (string, required). partition id, if the dataset is partitioned. In that case, use ALL to obtain metrics on the whole dataset. Example: 2015-02-03
Response details available: 200[body]

Resource: Dataset status
Summary: Retrieve the history values of a single metric on a given dataset (or dataset partition)
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/datasets/mydataset/metrics/history/?partition=2015-02-03?metricLookup=records:COUNT_RECORDS
Required privileges: Required privileges : READ_METADATA
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project. Example: MYPROJECT
- datasetName (string, required). the name of a dataset. Example: mydataset
- partition (string, required). partition id, if the dataset is partitioned. In that case, use ALL to obtain metrics on the whole dataset. Example: 2015-02-03
- metricLookup (string, required). id of the metric to get values for. Example: records:COUNT_RECORDS
Response details available: 200[body]

- POST /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/actions/updateFromHive :: Update from Hive metastore
- POST /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/actions/computeMetrics/{?partitions} :: Compute metrics

title: POST /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/actions/synchronizeHiveMetastore - Synchronize Hive metastore
text:
Endpoint: Synchronize Hive metastore
Method: POST
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/actions/synchronizeHiveMetastore
Module: Datasets
Resource: Actions on dataset
Summary: Synchronizes the Hive table associated with the dataset, that is, updates or create the table’s so that it corresponds to the dataset’s schema.
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/datasets/mydataset/actions/synchronizeHiveMetastore
Required privileges: Required privileges : READ_DATA
Note: Only makes sense on HDFS datasets
Response codes: 204
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project. Example: MYPROJECT
- datasetName (string, required). the name of a dataset. Example: mydataset
Response details available: 204[no-body]

title: POST /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/actions/updateFromHive - Update from Hive metastore
text:
Endpoint: Update from Hive metastore
Method: POST
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/{datasetName}/actions/updateFromHive
Module: Datasets
Resource: Actions on dataset
Summary: Updates the dataset from the table it’s associated with in Hive. This can change the dataset’s path, schema, partitioning scheme and format.
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/datasets/mydataset/actions/updateFromHive

title: GET /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/tables-import/actions/list-tables{?connectionName}{?catalogName}{?schemaName} - List tables
text:
Endpoint: List tables
Method: GET
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/datasets/tables-import/actions/list-tables{?connectionName}{?catalogName}{?schemaName}
Module: Datasets
Resource: Import tables
Summary: List tables in a schema of a SQL connection. If schema is empty, list tables in all schemas. If catalog is empty, list tables in all catalogs (aka databases for Snowflake and projects for BigQuery). Returns a reference to a future, defined by its jobId. If the future has no result yet, polling on the jobId until the result is ready is needed. The list elements are import candidates.
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/datasets/tables-import/actions/list-tables?connectionName=?catalogName=?schemaName=
Required privileges: Required privileges : WRITE_CONF
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project. Example: MYPROJECT
- connectionName (string, required). Name of the SQL connection in which to list tables. Use @virtual(hive-jdbc):hivedb for Hive
- catalogName (string, optional). Name of the catalog in the SQL connection in which to list tables
- schemaName (string, optional). Name of the schema in the SQL connection in which to list tables
Response details available: 200[body]

Module: Jobs

- GET /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{?limit} :: List latest jobs
- POST /projects/{projectKey}/jobs :: Run job

title: GET /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{?limit} - List latest jobs
text:
Endpoint: List latest jobs
Method: GET
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{?limit}
Module: Jobs
Resource: Jobs
Summary: Retrieves the list of the last jobs, as an array of JobSummary objects as defined in in the GET job call.
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/jobs/?limit=
Required privileges: Required privileges : READ_CONF
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project. Example: MYPROJECT
- limit (int, optional). Maximum number of returned jobs, 0 for no limit
Response details available: 200[headers,body,schema]

title: POST /projects/{projectKey}/jobs - Run job
text:
Endpoint: Run job
Method: POST
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/jobs
Module: Jobs
Resource: Jobs
Summary: Start building a list of outputs.
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/jobs
Required privileges: Required privileges : RUN_JOBS
Note: A successful call means that the job was successfully initilized, not that it is completed. To follow the build progress use GET job.
Note: Returns the complete job definition, including identifier of the job
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project. Example: MYPROJECT
Request includes: headers, body, schema
Response details available: 200[body,schema]

title: POST /projects/{projectKey}/jobs - response 200 schema
Jobs / Job resource overview
text:
Module: Jobs
Resource: Job
Resource ID: jobs-job
Endpoints in resource: 2
- GET /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{jobId} :: Get job status
- POST /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{jobId}/abort :: Abort

title: GET /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{jobId} - Get job status
text:
Endpoint: Get job status
Method: GET
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{jobId}
Module: Jobs
Resource: Job
Summary: Retrieves the job status as a JobSummary object summarising the state of a job and its activities.
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/jobs/build_something_2016_02_10_21_23_34
Required privileges: Required privileges : MONITOR_JOBS
Note: A DSS job is a sequence of operations performed on datasets.
Note: A job is divided into activities, each activity corresponds to a recipe run using a given set of partitions.
Note: Example : running two recipes on 2 partitions will result in a job with 4 activities.
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project. Example: MYPROJECT
- jobId (string, required). the id of a job. Example: build_something_2016_02_10_21_23_34
Response details available: 200[headers,body]

title: GET /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{jobId} - response 200 headers
text:
Endpoint response section: GET /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{jobId} - Get job status
Response status: 200
Section label: headers
Content-Type: application/json

title: GET /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{jobId} - response 200 body
POST /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{jobId}/abort - Abort
text:
Endpoint: Abort
Method: POST
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{jobId}/abort
Module: Jobs
Resource: Job
Summary: Requests the specified job to abort.
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/jobs/build_something_2016_02_10_21_23_34/abort
Required privileges: Required privileges : RUN_JOBS
Note: NB It may take some time for the job to actually stop, so when the servers answers this request, the job cannot be considered stopped.
Response codes: 204
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project. Example: MYPROJECT
- jobId (string, required). the id of a job. Example: build_something_2016_02_10_21_23_34
Response details available: 204[no-body]

title: Jobs / Job logs resource overview
text:

Resource ID: jobs-job-logs

- GET /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{jobId}/log{?activity} :: Get job logs

title: GET /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{jobId}/log{?activity} - Get job logs
text:
Endpoint: Get job logs
Method: GET
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/jobs/{jobId}/log{?activity}
Module: Jobs
Resource: Job logs
Summary: Retrieves the logs content for a job or one of its activities (if specified).
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/jobs/build_something_2016_02_10_21_23_34/log?activity=
Required privileges: Required privileges : READ_CONF
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project. Example: MYPROJECT
- jobId (string, required). the id of a job. Example: build_something_2016_02_10_21_23_34
- activity (string, optional). Activity id from which to output the logs. If not provided return the global job logs
Response details available: 200[headers]

Scenarios / Scenario resource overview
text:
Module: Scenarios
Resource: Scenario
Resource ID: scenarios-scenario
Endpoints in resource: 14
- GET /projects/{projectKey}/scenarios/{scenarioId}/ :: Get a scenario
- GET /projects/{projectKey}/scenarios/{scenarioId}/light :: Get the status of a scenario
- GET /projects/{projectKey}/scenarios/{scenarioId}/payload :: Get the payload of a scenario of a project
- DELETE /projects/{projectKey}/scenarios/{scenarioId}/ :: Delete scenario
- POST /projects/{projectKey}/scenarios/{scenarioId}/run :: Run a scenario
- POST /projects/{projectKey}/scenarios/{scenarioId}/abort :: Abort a scenario
- GET /projects/{projectKey}/scenarios/{scenarioId}/get-last-runs/{?limit} :: Get last runs

- GET /projects/{projectKey}/scenarios/{scenarioId}/{runId}/log{?stepId} :: Get scenario logs
- GET /projects/{projectKey}/scenarios/trigger/{scenarioId}/{triggerId}{?triggerRunId} :: Get a run of a trigger
- PUT /projects/{projectKey}/scenarios/{scenarioId} :: Update scenario settings
- PUT /projects/{projectKey}/scenarios/{scenarioId}/light :: Update basic scenario settings
- PUT /projects/{projectKey}/scenarios/{scenarioId}/payload :: Update scenario payload

PUT /projects/{projectKey}/scenarios/{scenarioId} - Update scenario settings
text:
Endpoint: Update scenario settings
Method: PUT
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/scenarios/{scenarioId}
Module: Scenarios
Resource: Scenario
Summary: Updates the settings of a Scenario.
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/scenarios/the_scenario
Required privileges: Required privileges : WRITE_CONF
Note: The [Scenario] object given as parameter in of a PUT call MUST have been previously obtained from a [GET scenario] call at the same URL.
Note: The object obtained with the GET method may contain undocumented attributes. You should not modify them as it could create an invalid state and thoses attributes may be removed in future releases without notice.
Response codes: 204

POST /projects/{projectKey}/models/lab/{analysisId}/{mlTaskId}/guess{?predictionType}{?targetVariable}{?timeVariable}{?timeseriesIdentifiers}{?fullReguess} - Re-guess a ML Task
text:
Endpoint: Re-guess a ML Task
Method: POST
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/models/lab/{analysisId}/{mlTaskId}/guess{?predictionType}{?targetVariable}{?timeVariable}{?timeseriesIdentifiers}{?fullReguess}
Module: Machine Learning - Lab
Resource: Machine learning lab - MLTask
Summary: Can be used to reguess all the settings of the ML task when no optional parameter are given, or (for prediction ML tasks only) to set a new value for a core parameter of the task (target variable or prediction type) and subsequently reguess the impacted settings.
Example URI: /projects/projectKey/models/lab/analysisId/mlTaskId/guess?predictionType=REGRESSION?targetVariable=?timeVariable=?timeseriesIdentifiers=?fullReguess=
Required privileges: Required privileges : WRITE_CONF on the project
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the identifier of a project
- analysisId (string, required). the identifier of a visual analysis
- mlTaskId (string, required). the identifier of the ML task
- predictionType (string, optional). Only valid for prediction tasks of either BINARY_CLASSIFICATION, MULTICLASS or REGRESSION type, ignored otherwise. The prediction type to set. Cannot be set if targetVariable, timeVariable or timeseriesIdentifiers is also specified. Choices: REGRESSION BINARY_CLASSIFICATION MULTICLASS. Example: REGRESSION
- targetVariable (string, optional). Only valid for prediction tasks, ignored for clustering. The target variable to set. Cannot be set if predictionType, timeVariable or timeseriesIdentifiers is also specified
- timeVariable (string, optional). Column to be used as time variable. Cannot be set if targetVariable, predictionType or timeseriesIdentifiers is also specified
- timeseriesIdentifiers (list of strings, optional). List of columns to be used as time series identifiers. Cannot be set if targetVariable, predictionType or timeVariable is also specified
- fullReguess (boolean, optional). Defaults to true. Scope of the reguess process: whether it should reguess all the settings after changing a core parameter, or only reguess impacted settings (e.g. target remapping when changing the target, metrics when changing the prediction type…). Ignored if no core paramater is given
Response details available: 200[headers,body]

title: POST /projects/{projectKey}/models/lab/{analysisId}/{mlTaskId}/reguess-with-forecasting-params - request headers
text:
Endpoint request section: POST /projects/{projectKey}/models/lab/{analysisId}/{mlTaskId}/reguess-with-forecasting-params - Change forecasting parameters of a time series forecasting ML Task and reguess impacted algorithm settings
Section label: headers
Content-Type: application/json

title: POST /projects/{projectKey}/models/lab/{analysisId}/{mlTaskId}/reguess-with-forecasting-params - request body
text:
Endpoint request section: POST /projects/{projectKey}/models/lab/{analysisId}/{mlTaskId}/reguess-with-forecasting-params - Change forecasting parameters of a time series forecasting ML Task and reguess impacted algorithm settings
Section label: body
+ Body: forecasting parameters
    + Content
        + forecastHorizon (number, optional) - Number of time steps within the forecast horizon
        + validationHorizons (number, optional) - The number of validation horizons to be set. If omitted, retains the previous ratio.
        + timestepParams (optional) - Time step parameters
            + Members
                + timeunit (string) - Valid time unit values are: `MILLISECOND`, `SECOND`, `MINUTE`, `HOUR`, `DAY`, `BUSINESS_DAY`, `WEEK`, `MONTH`, `QUARTER`, `HALF_YEAR`, `YEAR`.
                + numberOfTimeunits (number) - Number of time units within a time step.
                + endOfWeekDay (number) - The end of week day setting is only useful for the `WEEK` time unit. Valid values are: `1` (Sunday), `2` (Monday), ..., `7` (Saturday).
                + unitAlignment (number) - for each step, month index (between `1` and `3`) when `time_unit` is `QUARTER`, month index (between `1` and `6`) when `time_unit` is `HALF_YEAR`, month number (between `1` and `12`) when `time_unit` is `YEAR`.
                + monthlyAlignment (number) - for each step, day of the month (between `1` and `31`) when `time_unit` is `MONTH`, `QUARTER`, `HALF_YEAR` or `YEAR`.
        + updateAlgorithmSettings (boolean, optional) - Defaults to true. Whether the algorithm settings should be reguessed after changing time step parameters.

    + Example
             {
                "forecastHorizon": 1,
                "validationHorizons": 2,
                "timestepParams" : {
                    "timeunit": "DAY",
                    "numberOfTimeunits": 3,
                    "endOfWeekDay": 1
                 },
                "updateAlgorithmSettings" : true
            }

title: POST /projects/{projectKey}/savedmodels/{savedModelId}/versions/{versionId}{?codeEnvName}{?containerExecConfigName}{?folderRef}{?path} - Import MLflow version from file or path
endpoint_name: Import MLflow version from file or path
text:
Endpoint: Import MLflow version from file or path
Method: POST
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/savedmodels/{savedModelId}/versions/{versionId}{?codeEnvName}{?containerExecConfigName}{?folderRef}{?path}
Module: Machine Learning - Saved models
Resource: Saved model versions
Summary: Create a new version for this saved model from an uploaded MLflow model file or from a path containing a MLflow model in a managed folder. Requires the saved model to be created using Create saved model.
Example URI: /projects/projectKey/savedmodels/savedModelId/versions/versionId?codeEnvName=?containerExecConfigName=?folderRef=?path=
Required privileges: Required privileges : WRITE_CONF on the project
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). The key of the project
- savedModelId (string, required). Identifier of the saved model
- versionId (string, required). Version identifier
- codeEnvName (string, required). Name of the code env to use for this model version. The code env must contain at least mlflow and the package(s) corresponding to the used MLflow-compatible frameworks. If value is “INHERIT”, the code env currently defined for the project will be used
- containerExecConfigName (string, optional). Name of the containerized execution configuration to use while creating this model version. If value is “INHERIT”, the container execution configuration of the project will be used. If value is “NONE”, local execution will be used (no container)
- folderRef (string, optional). Identifier of the managed folder in the form PROJECT_KEY.MANAGED_FOLDER_ID
- path (string, optional). Path of the MLflow folder in the managed folder
Request includes: headers, body
Response details available: 200[no-body]

Endpoint: Generate model documentation from a custom template
Method: POST
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/savedmodels/{savedModelId}/versions/{versionId}/generate-documentation-from-custom-template
Module: Machine Learning - Saved models

Summary: Start the model documentation generation for this version using an attached template file to upload. Returns a reference to a future, defined by its jobId. - versionId (string, required). Version identifier

title: GET /projects/{projectKey}/runnables/{runnableType}/state/{run} - Poll state
text:
Endpoint: Poll state
Method: GET
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/runnables/{runnableType}/state/{run}
Module: Macros
Resource: Macro
Summary: Get the status of a run of the macro. If the macro is still running, then the result of the call contains true for running, and potentially a progress field with a stack of progress info. If the run is finished but failed, either resultError or storedError will contain details on the failure; otherwise the type field will be filled.
Example URI: /projects/MYPROJECT/runnables/MACRO_ID/state/RUN_ID
Required privileges: Required privileges : READ_CONF
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project. Example: MYPROJECT
- runnableType (string, required). the identifier of a macro. Example: MACRO_ID
- run (string, required). the identifier of the macro run. Example: RUN_ID
Response details available: 200[headers,body]

title: POST /projects/{projectKey}/apiservices/{serviceId}/packages/{packageId}/publish{?publishedServiceId} - Publish package
text:
Endpoint: Publish package
Method: POST
Path: /projects/{projectKey}/apiservices/{serviceId}/packages/{packageId}/publish{?publishedServiceId}
Module: API Services
Resource: API Services
Summary: Publish an API service package to the API Deployer.
Example URI: /projects/projectKey/apiservices/serviceId/packages/packageId/publish?publishedServiceId=
Required privileges: Required privileges : WRITE on the published API service if it pre-exists, the global Create published API services permission on the Deployer otherwise.
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, required). the key of a project
- serviceId (string, required). the id of a service in this project
- packageId (string, required). the id of a package in this service
- publishedServiceId (string, optional). the id of the published service where the package will be deployed (if not provided, will default to the service id) NB If no published service with the given id is found on the API Deployer, a new one will be created using this id
Response details available: 200[no-body]

title: POST /projectsFromBundle{?projectFolderId} - Create project from a bundle
text:
Endpoint: Create project from a bundle
Method: POST
Path: /projectsFromBundle{?projectFolderId}
Module: Bundles, Automation-side
Resource: New project
Summary: Creates a project from a bundle. The bundle Zip content must be sent using multipart, as a “file” part
Example URI: /projectsFromBundle?projectFolderId=KdLmPU6
Required privileges: Required privileges : ADMIN (global)
Response codes: 204
URI parameters:
- projectFolderId (string, optional). the ID of the project folder in which the new project will be created (if not provided, will defaults to root project folder) The projectFolderId can be found from the get project folder API call or in the URL when accessing DSS GUI.. Example: KdLmPU6
Response details available: 204[no-body]

title: POST /projectsFromBundle/fromArchive{?archivePath,projectFolderId} - Create project from a bundle
text:
Endpoint: Create project from a bundle
Method: POST
Path: /projectsFromBundle/fromArchive{?archivePath,projectFolderId}
Module: Bundles, Automation-side
Resource: New project
Summary: Creates a project from a bundle. The bundle Zip must already be present as a file on the Automation node host
Example URI: /projectsFromBundle/fromArchive?archivePath=&projectFolderId=KdLmPU6
Required privileges: Required privileges : ADMIN (global)
Response codes: 204
URI parameters:
- archivePath (string, required). Absolute path to the location of the bundle on the Automation node host
- projectFolderId (string, optional). the ID of the project folder in which the new project will be created (if not provided, will defaults to root project folder) The projectFolderId can be found from the get project folder API call or in the URL when accessing DSS GUI.. Example: KdLmPU6
Response details available: 204[no-body]

Summary: Uploads a bundle on the Deployer. If no project key is provided, the key of the bundle’s source project is used
Example URI: /project-deployer/projects/bundles?projectKey=MYPROJECT?filePart=/home/data/import/bundle-v1.zip
Required privileges: Required privileges : WRITE on the published project
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- projectKey (string, optional). the key of the project where the bundle is uploaded. If no published project matches this key, it is created. Example: MYPROJECT
- filePart (string, required). the absolute path where the bundle resides. Example: /home/data/import/bundle-v1.zip
Response details available: 200[headers,body,schema]

POST /admin/global-tag-categories - Create category
text:
Endpoint: Create category
Method: POST
Path: /admin/global-tag-categories
Module: DSS administration
Resource: Global tag categories
Summary: This call creates a new global tag category on the DSS instance.
Example URI: /admin/global-tag-categories
Required privileges: Required privileges : Admin
Note: Random colors are assigned to tags without specified colors.
Note: If appliesTo field is not provided, the created category is applied to all available types.
Note: appliesTo valid values are: PROJECT, DATASET, RECIPE, MANAGED_FOLDER, FLOW_ZONE, SAVED_MODEL, ANALYSIS, SQL_NOTEBOOK, JUPYTER_NOTEBOOK, ARTICLE, LAMBDA_SERVICE, SCENARIO, DASHBOARD, INSIGHT, WEB_APP, REPORT, STREAMING_ENDPOINT, STATISTICS_WORKSHEET and DATA_COLLECTION.
Response codes: 200

text:
Endpoint: Update category
Method: PUT
Path: /admin/global-tag-categories/{categoryName}
Module: DSS administration
Resource: Global tag category
Summary: Update one or more properties of a global tag category.
Example URI: /admin/global-tag-categories/categoryName
Required privileges: Required privileges : Admin
Note: You can rename a category by supplying a name field in the body with a value different from the one in the parameters.
Note: If globalTags field is omitted, the tags are not updated. Otherwise, the supplied tags overwrite the existing tags.
Note: If appliesTo field is omitted, the value of appliesTo is not updated. Otherwise, it is overwritten with the provided values.
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- categoryName (string, required). the name of a global tag category in DSS

title: DELETE /admin/global-tag-categories/{?deletionMode}{?reassignTo} - Delete category
text:
Endpoint: Delete category
Method: DELETE
Path: /admin/global-tag-categories/{?deletionMode}{?reassignTo}
Module: DSS administration
Resource: Global tag category
Summary: Delete an existing global tag category.
Example URI: /admin/global-tag-categories/?deletionMode=?reassignTo=
Required privileges: Required privileges : Admin
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- deletionMode (string, optional). Specify how tagged objects are updated: KEEP keeps the tags on tagged objects. They will just not appear as global tags anymore. REMOVE removes the tags from tagged objects. REASSIGN moves all tags to another category and update the tagged objects accordingly
- reassignTo (string, optional). When deletionMode=REASSIGN, name of the other category to merge with the deleted one
Response details available: 200[headers]

title: DELETE /admin/global-tag-categories/tags/{?deletionMode}{?reassignTo} - Delete tag
text:
Endpoint: Delete tag
Method: DELETE
Path: /admin/global-tag-categories/tags/{?deletionMode}{?reassignTo}
Module: DSS administration
Resource: Global tag
Summary: Delete a tag from a global tag category.
Example URI: /admin/global-tag-categories/tags/?deletionMode=?reassignTo=
Required privileges: Required privileges : Admin
Response codes: 200
URI parameters:
- deletionMode (string, optional). Specify how tagged objects are updated: KEEP keeps the tags on tagged objects. They will just not appear as global tags anymore. REMOVE removes the tags from tagged objects. REASSIGN moves all tags to another category and update the tagged objects accordingly
- reassignTo (string, optional). When deletionMode=REASSIGN, name of the other tag to merge with the deleted one
Response details available: 200[headers]
